violations.text: Per interview of staff, slicers are only washed, rinsed, and sanitized at the end of the day. The surfaces of equipment that contacts time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as meats and cheeses must be washed, rinsed, and sanitized at least every four hours. Slicer taken out of service. Proper sanitizing procedures reviewed with PIC. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
Sliced deli meat was observed held past a day or date that exceeds 7 days. Product disposed of. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026
Working containers used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies were not marked with the common name of the chemical. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

Quaternary ammonium sanitizer solution was in excess of 400 parts per million (ppm). A quaternary ammonia sanitizer solution applied to food contact surfaces must be applied at the strength as indicated on the manufacturers' label. Sani bottles were emptied and filled with appropriate concentration. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/16/2026

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety managers certification course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety managers course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety certification course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety course. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024

Food was observed held at 45-54 degrees Fahrenheit in the walk-in cooler. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per person in charge, food had been in the walk-in cooler for more than 4 hours. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). The ambient air temperature was observed at 47 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Walk-in cooler was taken out of service and must not be used until it can maintain an internal food temperature of 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below. Walk-in cooler must be repaired/replaced/adjusted. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/08/2024

Establishment did not have a person in charge that has successfully completed an approved food safety manager certification course. Please email inspector proof that additional employees have been enrolled and completed an approved food safety course. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024

Food was observed held at 50-52 degrees Fahrenheit in the display case. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Food was voluntarily disposed of (see attached disposal form). Display case had an ambient air temperature of 54 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. The temperature was adjusted during time of inspection and the temperature dropped to 41 degrees Fahrenheit during time of inspection. Display case must be monitored. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/07/2024
Food was observed stored on the floor in the walk-in cooler. Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/23/2024
